summary: Well-structured shared meta blueprint defining a four-layer separation (concept → scenarios → implementationPlan → contracts) with clear precedence, per-layer question inventories, quality attributes with dual .means() definitions, and causal degradation chains. Strong local recoverability and self-description. Main flaw: hardcoded product file paths in implementationPlan.contains() break shared_meta abstraction purity by coupling a reusable layering framework to specific product artifacts. Some Quality .means() definitions restate what the attribute name already activates, slightly diluting density.
strengths: Layer definitions with decides/avoids pairs create tight, locally recoverable semantics
Dual .means() calls on quality attributes reduce interpretive slack by providing complementary perspectives
when/then degradation chains clearly model what goes wrong when layers weaken or compression is misapplied
Layer.answers() blocks give concrete, actionable question inventories that anchor each layer's scope
prescribes() block consolidates writing norms without redundancy against the language root
weaknesses: implementationPlan.contains() hardcodes specific product file paths (agentish-graph.code-architecture.ts, agentish-graph.operational-behavior.ts), coupling shared meta to product artifacts and creating staleness risk
Some Quality .means() definitions (e.g. recoverableStructure, exactNativeSchemas) largely restate what the attribute name already makes obvious, reducing net density
No explicit versioning or scope declaration for which product domains this layering applies to, leaving corpus role slightly ambiguous
